_comment: Shared, version-controlled az-tool-guard policy. defaultMode='prompt' means an unmatched call triggers an interactive prompt (TTY) or auto-deny (non-TTY). Per-user overrides go in .tool-guard/az.config.local.json (gitignored, populated by the prompt's 'save' actions). Cross-cutting rules (e.g. * delete *, * purge *) live in .tool-guard/_defaults.json — only az-specific deny rules are listed here. Precedence: deny > warn > allow > defaultMode. See scripts/tool-guard/az/POLICY.md for design notes.

deny.message: az logout would clear the credential cache used by the git credential helper for dev.azure.com — fetches and pushes against the org would start prompting. Run 'az account clear' explicitly only if you really intend to break the helper.
Same effect as 'az logout' — clears the credential cache used by git's HTTPS auth against dev.azure.com.
Switching the active subscription silently changes which environment subsequent 'az' commands target. Be explicit by passing --subscription on each command instead.
az config set persists changes to the global az config file. Prefer per-command flags (--output, --only-show-errors) so other tools aren't affected.
Deleting a service principal usually breaks production deployments using its credentials. Coordinate with the team and rotate dependent secrets before deletion.
Deleting a Key Vault secret can break running workloads. Soft-delete is enabled — recovery is possible within the retention window, but PURGE is not. Coordinate with the team.
PURGE is irreversible — bypasses soft-delete entirely. Almost never the right command. If you must, do it from the Azure portal where the action is auditable.
Resource group deletion cascades to every resource inside it. This is the most blast-radius operation in Azure CLI. Verify the group name twice and confirm with the team.
Cancelling a pipeline run can leave deployments half-applied (some stages succeeded, others didn't). Prefer waiting for the run to finish, then rolling back via a follow-up commit.
Abandoning a PR closes it without merging. Coordinate with the PR author before abandoning their work.
